method: interactive verification run (design/flow simulation); web search cross-check after direct fetches of openai.com and theverge.com were blocked by bot protection

claims.text: OpenAI released GPT-Live on 8 July 2026 and it now powers ChatGPT Voice
claims.reasoning: Primary source (Tier 0, OpenAI News feed item + announcement page) plus independent same-day coverage by The Verge and TechCrunch.

claims.text: Two models ship at launch: GPT-Live-1 for paid tiers, GPT-Live-1-mini for free users, on iOS, Android and the web
claims.reasoning: Stated by OpenAI; tier/platform split reported consistently by TechCrunch and 9to5Mac.

claims.text: GPT-Live is full-duplex — it listens and speaks simultaneously and handles interruptions without restarting
claims.reasoning: Capability described by OpenAI; press coverage attributes it to OpenAI ('it says'). No independent benchmark or systematic hands-on test found at publication time. Independence collapse applies: all descriptions trace to the single OpenAI announcement.

claims.text: OpenAI says complex queries are handed off to a frontier model in the background, currently GPT-5.5
claims.reasoning: Claim is about what OpenAI stated (not about measured behavior); statement present in the announcement and quoted by TechCrunch.

notes: Watch item: flip the full-duplex capability claim to confirmed if/when independent hands-on testing or latency measurements appear.
